channel.title: Comments for page "Administrative Leave"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Administrative Leave"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alexylva and Anderson feel shallow, here; I get less of a feeling of Alexylva and more a myriad of unnecessary reminders half-baked into the prose. Similarly, there's little to distinguish Anderson from any other "robots are taking over" deal.

While I never felt like the piece was dragging (and good on you!), the prose also felt a little bland. Baking how your PoV character thinks into the narrative is a good way to both distinguish your prose and characterize without speaking; however, given that I tend to write a ton of stream of conscious, I might be biased here.

item.title: Site Criticism Team - Review
item.content:encoded:
Noticed this hasn't been getting much response.

+[Critique] Click to Access
-[Click to Hide Text]

Adjusting his glasses to the tip of his nose, Mago ran a finger down the cold stone tablets, stopping once he saw his name: Mago, Fulvius, assigned to a new course. AN/AX-054, “The Pinocchio Question,” alongside one “Hannibal Locke.” How peculiar.

What on earth is peculiar about this?

I found this really hard to follow. Things seem to move a bit fast at places, making the parts that are further elaborated on difficult to understand. The introduction to the machines just kind of happens, while I think you meant to bring it in slowly. I don't really get the Anderson connection until the piece ends, either, and even then its blurry.

Aside from that, the writing was middling in performance. I feel like some of the language gets unnecessarily flowery at points and distracts from the scene. It's mostly fluff, in my opinion.

This was a downvote for me. I think this could have done better if there had been more time spent elaborating on the Anderson involvement rather than Mago's time at Alexylva.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to RockTeethMothEyes or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: "Author" Post
item.content:encoded: The bulk of this Tale was penned by my collaborator, Popsioak. I provided some editing and emotional support. Written for Collabcon 2019. Tags incoming.
